intro: The biggest practical difference between Bearer and Codacy is lock-in. Bearer is rated high lock-in (oss data security scanner), while Codacy is rated medium lock-in (code quality reports exportable). That changes how hard a future migration would be and how much strategy to put into exit planning. Beyond the headline, Bearer carries high lock-in (oss data security scanner) and freemium; Codacy carries medium lock-in (code quality reports exportable) and freemium with paid plans from $0. Bearer is a fit when you are mobile. Codacy is a fit when you are mobile. The honest trade-off: Bearer is explicitly not for hobby projects; Codacy is explicitly not for hobby projects. On the product side, Bearer is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Data Flow Security, and Codacy is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Code Quality. Common Bearer use cases include vulnerability scanning in ci/cd. Common Codacy use cases include vulnerability scanning in ci/cd.
quick_take: Bearer is for mobile; Codacy is for mobile; decide on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Bearer if you accept higher lock-in in exchange for its specific capabilities and you are mobile.
verdict_b: Choose Codacy if its medium lock-in is acceptable for your use case and you are mobile.

faq.a: Both tools use freemium pricing. Bearer is listed as freemium; Codacy as freemium with paid plans from $0. Without specific volume and seat assumptions it is not possible to say which is cheaper in general.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Bearer to Codacy?
faq.a: Migration from Bearer is harder: Bearer is rated high lock-in (oss data security scanner). You can still move, but expect schema-mapping, export tooling and a longer cutover window. Budget engineering time accordingly.

Both tools tie at 4/5 for developer experience on our scoring. The real difference will show up in your specific workflow, not the headline rating. A one-hour spike on both is the fastest way to decide.

faq.q: Is Codacy a good alternative to Bearer?
faq.a: Codacy targets the same best-fit audience as Bearer (mobile). Note that Codacy is explicitly not for hobby projects, so it only replaces Bearer if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (high vs medium), which is the main thing to weigh.
